title: trachymene coerulea
intro: Trachymene coerulea, also known as Blue Mitchell or Blue Tansy, is a flowering plant native to eastern Australia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to repel insects and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The health benefits of Trachymene coerulea include its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as an essential oil or dried herb, often combined with other ingredients in teas, salves, and ointments for its medicinal and therapeutic effects.
